title: Amazon Creative Ops Platform for Sellers
description: Expanded Genrupt from a fragile AI media MVP into a broader Amazon creative ops platform. Built subscription and organization billing, queue-backed generation workflows, Canva-like text editing, and architecture foundations for market analysis, listing builder, A+ content, A/B testing, and MCP-based agent workflows.
problem: Genrupt had outgrown its original AI image and video MVP. To support paying customers, it needed real billing, safer credit accounting, and more reliable handling for long-running image, video, and storyboard jobs.

At the same time, the product surface was expanding into Amazon seller workflows like market analysis, variations, listing builder, A+ content, and experimentation, which put pressure on architecture, state management, and async infrastructure.
solution: Built subscription, organization, and credit-ledger infrastructure around Stripe, including pricing, checkout, webhook reconciliation, tier changes, cancellation/reactivation handling, and org-to-individual transition logic.

Migrated long-running generation flows onto pg-boss workers and hardened the async engine so image, video, storyboard, and related jobs could retry safely without drifting billing or leaving users in contradictory states.

Shipped a Canva-like text editing workflow for product images: OCR detects text, AI inpainting removes it, and users replace it inline with cleaner controls, batch-aware flows, and brand-aware styling.

Led a broad architecture cleanup across routes, services, and client state: moved app-to-app calls behind server actions and shared services, standardized React Query and Zustand usage, and reduced the retained API surface from 393 routes to 177.

Helped prepare the platform for newer Amazon seller workflows and agent usage by working across market analysis, variations, listing builder, A+ tooling, and the MCP layer that exposes a typed public agent interface.
result: Moved Genrupt from a fragile creative MVP toward a billable Amazon creative platform with production subscription flows, org billing, and safer cost accounting.

Made long-running product workflows materially more reliable across storyboard, variations, listing builder, market analysis, and A+ by tightening worker lifecycle, persistence, and job recovery behavior.
Load-testing work on the newer architecture set current planning numbers at a 90-user webinar burst and 300-user future-planning scenario, giving the team clearer launch capacity targets.
The codebase is easier to extend: cleaner boundaries, fewer internal route hops, and a real MCP surface make it more practical to keep shipping new seller workflows and agent-driven features.
